author: Augustine of Hippo

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XIII. 26–31.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I Know, dearly beloved, that some may be moved, as the godly to inquire into the meaning of, and the ungodly to find fault with, the statement, that it was after the Lord had given the bread, that had been dipped, to His betrayer that Satan entered into him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For so it is written: “And when He had dipped the bread, He gave it to Judas Iscariot, the Son of Simon.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And after the bread, then entered Satan into him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they say, Was this the worth of Christ’s bread, given from Christ’s own table, that after it Satan should enter into His disciple?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the answer we give them is, that thereby we are taught rather how much we need to beware of receiving what is good in a sinful spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the point of special importance is, not the thing that is received, but the person that receives it; and not the character of the thing that is given, but of him to whom it is given.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For even good things are hurtful, and evil things are beneficial, according to the character of the recipients.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Sin,” says the apostle, “that it might appear sin, wrought death to me by that which is good.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, you see, evil is brought about by the good, so long as that which is good is wrongly received.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is he also that says: “Lest I should be exalted unduly through the greatness of my revelations, there was given to me a thorn in my flesh, the messenger of Satan to buffet me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For which thing I besought the Lord thrice, that He would take it away from me; and He said unto me, My grace is sufficient for thee: for strength is made perfect in weakness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And here, you see, good was brought about by that which was evil, when the evil was received in a good spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, do we wonder if Christ’s bread was given to Judas, that thereby he should be made over to the devil; when we see, on the other hand, that Paul was visited by a messenger of the devil, that by such an instrumentality he might be perfected in Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this way, both the good was injurious to the evil man, and the evil was beneficial to the good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Bear in mind the meaning of the Scripture, “Whosoever shall eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord unworthily, shall be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when the apostle said this, he was dealing with those who were taking the body of the Lord, like any other food, in an undiscerning and careless spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, he is thus taken to task who does not discern, that is, does not distinguish from the other kinds of food, the body of the Lord, what condemnation must be his, who in the guise of a friend comes as an enemy to His table!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If negligence in the guest is thus visited with blame, what must be the punishment that will fall on the man that sells the very person who has invited him to his table!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And why was the bread given to the traitor, but as an evidence of the grace he had treated with ingratitude?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was after this bread, then, that Satan entered into the Lord’s betrayer, that, as now given over to his power, he might take full possession of one into whom before this he had only entered in order to lead him into error.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we are not to suppose that he was not in him when he went to the Jews and bargained about the price of betraying the Lord; for the evangelist Luke very plainly attests this when he says: “Then entered Satan into Judas, who was surnamed Iscariot, being one of the twelve; and he went his way, and communed with the chief priests.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here, you see, it is shown that Satan had already entered into Judas.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His first entrance, therefore, was when he implanted in his heart the thought of betraying Christ; for in such a spirit had he already come to the supper.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now, after the bread, he entered into him, no longer to tempt one who belonged to another, but to take possession of him as his own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it was not then, as some thoughtless readers suppose, that Judas received the body of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we are to understand that the Lord had already dispensed to all of them the sacrament of His body and blood, when Judas also was present, as very clearly related by Saint Luke; and it was after this that we come to the moment when, in accordance with John’s account, the Lord made a full disclosure of His betrayer by dipping and holding out to him the morsel of bread, and intimating perhaps by the dipping of the bread the false pretensions of the other.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the dipping of a thing does not always imply its washing; but some things are dipped in order to be dyed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if a good meaning is to be here attached to the dipping, his ingratitude for that good was deservedly followed by damnation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But still, possessed as Judas now was, not by the Lord, but by the devil, and now that the bread had entered the belly, and an enemy the soul of this man of ingratitude: still, I say, there was this enormous wickedness, already conceived in his heart, waiting to be wrought out to its full issue, for which the damnable desire had always preceded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, when the Lord, the living Bread, had given this bread to the dead, and in giving it had revealed the betrayer of the Bread, He said, “What thou doest, do quickly.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not command the crime, but foretold evil to Judas, and good to us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what could be worse for Judas, or what could be better for us, than the delivering up of Christ,—a deed done by him to his own destruction, but done, apart from him, in our behalf?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “What thou doest, do quickly.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Oh that word of One whose wish was to be ready rather than to be angry!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That word! expressing not so much the punishment of the traitor as the reward awaiting the Redeemer!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He said, “What thou doest, do quickly,” not as wrathfully looking to the destruction of the trust-betrayer, but in His own haste to accomplish the salvation of the faithful; for He was delivered for our offences, and He loved the Church, and gave Himself for it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And as the apostle also says of himself: “Who loved me, and gave Himself for me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Had not, then, Christ given Himself, no one could have given Him up.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is there in Judas’ conduct but sin?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in delivering up Christ he had no thought of our salvation, for which Christ was really delivered, but thought only of his money gain, and found the loss of his soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He got the wages he wished, but had also given him, against his wish, the wages he merited.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Judas delivered up Christ, Christ delivered Himself up: the former transacted the business of his own selling of his Master, the latter the business of our redemption.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “What thou doest, do quickly,” not because thou hast the power in thyself, but because He wills it who has all the power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Now no one of those at the table knew for what intent He spake this unto him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For some of them thought, because Judas had the money-bag, that Jesus said unto him, Buy those things which we have need of against the feast; or, that he should give something to the poor.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, therefore, had also a money-box, where He kept the offerings of believers, and distributed to the necessities of His own, and to others who were in need.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was then that the custom of having church-money was first introduced, so that thereby we might understand that His precept about taking no thought for the morrow was not a command that no money should be kept by His saints, but that God should not be served for any such end, and that the doing of what is right should not be held in abeyance through the fear of want.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the apostle also has this foresight for the future, when he says: “If any believer hath widows, let him give them enough, that the church may not be burdened, that it may have enough for them that are widows indeed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He then, having received the morsel of bread, went immediately out: and it was night.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he that went out was himself the night.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore when” the night “was gone out, Jesus said, Now is the Son of man glorified.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The day therefore uttered speech unto the day, that is, Christ did so to His faithful disciples, that they might hear and love Him as His followers; and the night showed knowledge unto the night, that is, Judas did so to the unbelieving Jews, that they might come as His persecutors, and make Him their prisoner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now, in considering these words of the Lord, which were addressed to the godly, before His arrest by the ungodly, special attention on the part of the hearer is required; and therefore it will be more becoming in the preacher, instead of hurriedly considering them now, to defer them till a future occasion.
